title: Tests not isolated from user cache/network: search-DB download breaks no_db tests since v0.3.0
description: The v0.3.0 release published the search-DB asset, so the download path in _resolve_search_db now succeeds for real during tests. cli_test::test_search_subcommand_no_db downloads the DB into ~/.cache/python-lucide (exit 0 instead of 1) and pollutes later no_db tests in search_test.py. All CI test jobs fail since the release. Fix: autouse conftest fixture pointing _get_cache_dir at tmp_path and blocking urllib.request.urlretrieve.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #143: conftest autouse fixture isolates cache dir and blocks downloads

title: Deploy Search App workflow fails: cp into root-owned _site
description: actions/jekyll-build-pages runs in a Docker container as root, so _site is root-owned. The 'Merge search app into site' step (cp -r docs/search _site/search) fails with 'Permission denied' on every main push since #140. Fix: run the copy with sudo.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #143: sudo the cp into root-owned _site

title: Apply code-review fixes to feat/semantic-search
description: High-effort code review found 10 issues: stale-cluster workflow gap, web artifacts hygiene, bogus --search-only error msgs, hardcoded cluster DB path, unguarded optional imports, empty ordered_names IndexError, fail-silent metadata population, missing get_icon_description export, duplicated scripts/, double metadata read. Fixing all on feat/semantic-search.
close_reason: All 10 review findings fixed; tests and hooks green; pushed

title: Search app: align header with content container, add curated example prompts
description: (1) Header brand/nav are full-bleed while content is capped at 1120px — align header inner content to the same container. (2) Add example query chips under the empty search bar to teach sentence-style queries; candidates evaluated offline against both MiniLM and bge-small embeddings, keeping only prompts with strong top results on both.

title: Search app UI polish: footer link, GitHub icon, model info popover, simpler theme toggle
description: Post-launch feedback: (1) footer 'python-lucide' should link to the repo and read 'powered by'; (2) '<>' header icon is obscure — use the GitHub mark; (3) add an info popover explaining the Faster/Better model toggle; (4) theme cycle system→light→dark has a visually dead 'system' step that reads as broken — make it a plain light/dark toggle seeded from the OS preference.

title: Cluster naming leaks Gemini chain-of-thought into theme names
description: CLUSTER_NAMING_MODEL is gemini-2.5-flash, a thinking model; name_clusters() in src/lucide/build_clusters.py accepts the raw response text with no validation, so chain-of-thought sometimes leaks into theme names. Current committed lucide-icon-clusters.json has 2 of 90 themes affected (clusters 14 and 67: 'THINK\n...' / 'THOUGHTS:...', up to ~3,960 chars). The web app truncates with CSS ellipsis so the damage there is cosmetic, but the strings ship in the search DB and clusters JSON.
acceptance_criteria: name_clusters validates themes (length cap, no newlines), retries once on failure, then falls back to 'Cluster {lid}'; consider disabling thinking via generationConfig.thinkingConfig.thinkingBudget=0 in _call_gemini_text; clusters 14 and 67 repaired in lucide-icon-clusters.json; search DB rebuilt
notes: Found during feat/semantic-search-web rebase 2026-06-09; the web branch had previously hand-patched one leaked theme instead of fixing the generator.

title: Cache embeddings by text hash to skip redundant re-embedding
description: make search-data runs build-search twice by design (clustering needs embeddings from the first pass; second pass bakes in fresh clusters), so all ~1,713 documents are embedded twice per pipeline run even though the embedding texts are identical between passes. Cache embeddings keyed by a hash of the embedding text (e.g. a table in the search DB or a sidecar cache file): on rebuild, only embed texts whose hash is new/changed. Second pass then becomes near-instant, and weekly icon updates only embed the handful of new/changed descriptions. Touches build_search_db() in src/lucide/build_search.py.

title: Parallelize cluster naming with a thread pool
description: name_clusters() in src/lucide/build_clusters.py makes ~90 sequential Gemini calls (one per cluster), which dominated the search-data pipeline wall time (~8 of ~12 minutes). generate_descriptions() in build_search.py already uses concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor with _DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY=20 for the same kind of urllib Gemini calls — apply the same pattern to cluster naming. Should cut the naming phase from minutes to seconds.

title: Multilingual embedding model for non-English icon search
description: MiniLM-L6 and bge-small-en-v1.5 (web) and nomic-embed-text-v1.5 (Python) are English-only, so queries like 'herz', 'coeur', 'corazón' fail. Add a multilingual model — e.g. multilingual-e5-small (~100MB, 384d, ONNX-friendly, needs 'query:'/'passage:' prefixes) — as a third browser option and/or as the Python-side model. Unlike adding bigger English models, this adds a genuine capability rather than a marginal score bump. Measure with scripts/test_search_quality.py extended with non-English queries before adopting. Pairs with the existing lightweight-WASM-model idea (bd memory). Touches web/scripts/compute-embeddings.mjs MODEL_CONFIGS, web model-loader, and optionally src/lucide/config.py DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL.

title: Experiment: synthetic queries per icon to boost search recall
description: Currently each icon gets one 30-60 word VLM description embedded as a single document. A known stronger retrieval pattern is generating several short query-like strings per icon (e.g. for loader-circle: 'loading', 'please wait', 'progress', 'spinner') and embedding those alongside or instead of the prose description — often improves recall because the documents live in the same register as real user queries. Measure against the current pipeline with scripts/test_search_quality.py before adopting. Touches build_search.py (_embedding_text / generation prompt) and possibly the search DB schema (multiple vectors per icon, max-score aggregation at query time).

comments.author: Mike Macpherson
comments.text:
Fully client-side semantic icon search running in the browser via WASM. No server, no API keys, no backend.

## Architecture

- Pre-computed embeddings for all 1,703 icons shipped as a static binary file (~2-5MB depending on model/dimensions)
- Small embedding model compiled to WASM runs in the browser to embed the users query
- Cosine similarity computed client-side (trivial at 1,703 vectors)
- Static site deployable to GitHub Pages

## Embedding model for WASM

Need a model small enough to download + run in-browser. Candidates:
- all-MiniLM-L6-v2: ~23MB quantized ONNX, 384d. onnxruntime-web is mature for this.
- gte-small: ~67MB, 384d. Better quality but larger download.
- A Rust-native model via candle (HF Rust ML framework) compiled to WASM -- potentially leaner than ONNX-in-WASM.

Could offer multiple quality tiers: a fast/small default that loads instantly, plus an option to load a larger model for better results.

## Multi-embedding comparison

Pre-compute embeddings with several models (nomic, MiniLM, gte-small) and let users compare search quality side-by-side. This becomes a compelling demo/blog post showing how model choice affects retrieval.

## Key questions

- Rust+candle WASM vs onnxruntime-web? candle is leaner but less mature for WASM.
- Quantization: int8 or even int4 for the WASM model to keep download small?
- Can we ship the icon SVGs inline so the whole demo is a single HTML file?
- Progressive loading: show results from pre-computed popular queries instantly, then load the full model for custom queries?

## Related

- python-lucide-a2l (web app) -- this is the zero-dependency public demo version
- The pre-computed embeddings from the search DB are reusable; only the query-side model needs to run in WASM

comments.author: Mike Macpherson
comments.text:
## Updated plan: Transformers.js + multi-model embeddings

Fastest path is Transformers.js (HuggingFace JS library, runs ONNX models via WASM in-browser). No Rust/candle needed.

### Architecture

- Single static HTML file, deployable to GitHub Pages
- Transformers.js loaded from CDN (~200KB)
- Default model: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 quantized int8 (~23MB, cached after first visit)
- Optional upgrade: nomic-embed-text-v1.5 (~130MB, toggle in UI, also cached)
- Pre-computed embedding files per model:
  - embeddings-minilm.bin (1,703 x 384 x 4 = 2.6MB)
  - embeddings-nomic.bin (1,703 x 768 x 4 = 5.2MB)
- Icon SVGs inline or as sprite (~800KB)
- Descriptions and icons are model-independent, shared

### Build changes needed

- Add --model flag to lucide build-search to generate embeddings for different models
- Export embeddings as raw binary files (not SQLite) for web consumption
- JSONL descriptions stay the same across all models

### UX

- First visit: ~26MB download (MiniLM), 2-3s to interactive
- Subsequent visits: instant (browser Cache API)
- Dropdown to switch models, side-by-side comparison mode
- Could demo "madrone" with MiniLM vs nomic to show quality difference

### Blog post angle

Multi-model comparison showing how embedding model choice affects semantic search quality, all running live in the reader's browser.

title: Interactive web app for browsing and searching icons

comments.author: Mike Macpherson
comments.text:
A lucide serve command that starts a local web app for browsing and searching icons.

Key features:
- Semantic search (type a query, get ranked icon results with descriptions)
- Browse by discovered cluster/theme
- Filter by Lucide tags and categories
- Click an icon to see SVG, copy markup, view description and cluster membership
- Possibly the UMAP embedding visualization as an interactive page

Design questions to resolve:
- Framework: Starlette, FastHTML+Datastar, Flask, or something else? Should match the projects ethos (lightweight, modern Python). Ships as part of the search extra.
- Architecture: server-rendered HTML (htmx/datastar style) vs JSON API + lightweight JS? SSE for streaming search results?
- Dependency: only pulled in with pip install python-lucide[search] or a separate extra like [web]?
- Data: serves from the relational SQLite DBs (main + search), no vector index needed at this scale

title: Multi-level and soft clustering for icon taxonomy

comments.author: Mike Macpherson
comments.text:
## Context

The current HDBSCAN clustering produces 88 hard-assigned clusters (each icon in exactly one group). This works well for visualization and theme discovery, but misses two important capabilities:

## 1. Soft / Multi-membership Clustering

Icons naturally belong to multiple themes. A heart icon relates to emotions, social, health, and gaming simultaneously. Two approaches:

Cosine similarity to cluster centroids: Compute the mean embedding per cluster, then for each icon report all clusters where similarity exceeds a threshold. Simple, data-driven, produces a weighted tag-like structure.

BERTopic / topic modeling: Designed for soft assignments -- gives each document a probability distribution over topics. Would produce per-icon topic probabilities that could be thresholded into multi-membership tags.

## 2. Hierarchical / Multi-level Clustering

The 88 clusters likely have natural super-groups. For example Directional Arrows, Chevron Navigation, Corner Arrows, and Sorting and Ordering could all fall under a parent Navigation and Direction theme.

Approaches: agglomerative clustering on cluster centroids, HDBSCAN condensed tree (already computes hierarchy internally), or LLM-assisted grouping of the 88 theme names into 8-12 super-themes.

## Output

A richer lucide-icon-clusters.json with per-icon theme weights (soft membership), per-theme parent super-theme (hierarchy), and new search API methods like search_icons_by_theme and get_icon_themes.

title: Kitty graphics protocol in tmux
description: Inline icon rendering works in Ghostty/kitty directly but cursor positioning is unreliable through tmux passthrough. Disabled for now.

value: Mike wants a lightweight WASM-compatible embedding model as a second option for browser-side Lucide icon search without the 45MB fastembed/Nomic download. Deferred to a future PR after feat/semantic-search merges.
